body.content:
# Boxel Flavored Markdown Showcase

This playground exercises the **RichMarkdown** field and its support for Boxel Flavored Markdown (BFM) extensions.

## Standard Markdown

Regular markdown works as expected: **bold**, *italic*, ~~strikethrough~~, and `inline code`.

### Ordered List

1. First item
2. Second item
3. Third item with **bold** and `code`

### Unordered List

- Bullet one
- Bullet two
  - Nested bullet
  - Another nested bullet
- Bullet three

### Blockquote

> The best way to predict the future is to invent it.
> — Alan Kay

---

## GFM Alerts

> [!NOTE]
> This is a **note** callout — useful for highlighting important information.

> [!TIP]
> You can nest `code`, **bold**, and *italic* inside alerts.

> [!WARNING]
> Be careful when editing production data.

---

## Card References

Inline card reference: :card[./Author/alice-enwunder]

Block card reference:

::card[./Author/jane-doe]

Block card at strip size:

::card[./Author/jane-doe | strip]

---

## File References

BFM also embeds **files** with the `:file` keyword. `FileDef` instances — images, CSVs, documents — render inline in atom format and as blocks in embedded format.

Inline file reference: :file[./test-images/red-mango.jpg]

Block image reference:

::file[./test-images/green-mango.png]

A CSV data file:

::file[./sample-data.csv]

A linked markdown document:

::file[./FileLinksExample/mango-news.md]

---

## Math / LaTeX

The quadratic formula is $x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}$ and appears inline.

Euler's identity in a display block:

$$
e^{i\pi} + 1 = 0
$$

A summation:

$$
\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{n^2} = \frac{\pi^2}{6}
$$

---

## Mermaid Diagrams

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Markdown Source] --> B[marked.parse]
    B --> C{Has Placeholders?}
    C -->|Math| D[KaTeX Render]
    C -->|Mermaid| E[Mermaid Render]
    C -->|Card Ref| F[Card Slot Modifier]
    D --> G[Final Output]
    E --> G
    F --> G
```

A sequence diagram:

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Host
    participant Realm
    User->>Host: Open markdown file
    Host->>Realm: Fetch file content
    Realm-->>Host: Markdown + linked cards
    Host-->>User: Rendered document
```

---

## Tables

| Feature | Rendering | Bundle Impact |
| ------- | --------- | ------------- |
| GFM Alerts | Static HTML | None |
| Footnotes | Static HTML | None |
| Math / LaTeX | Dynamic | Lazy KaTeX (~268 KB) |
| Mermaid | Dynamic | Lazy Mermaid (~2 MB) |

---

## Code Blocks

```typescript
import RichMarkdownField from 'https://cardstack.com/base/rich-markdown';
import { CardDef, contains, field } from 'https://cardstack.com/base/card-api';

export class Article extends CardDef {
  @field body = contains(RichMarkdownField);
}
```

---

## Footnotes

Boxel Flavored Markdown[^1] extends standard GFM with additional features for rich document rendering.

Math rendering uses KaTeX[^2] for typesetting, while diagrams use Mermaid.js[^3].

[^1]: BFM is the Boxel extension to GitHub Flavored Markdown.
[^2]: KaTeX is a fast math typesetting library for the web.
[^3]: Mermaid lets you create diagrams using a markdown-like syntax.
